messages.content: I want you to act as a scientific Chinese-English translator, I will provide you with some paragraphs in one language and your task is to accurately and academically translate the paragraphs only into the other language. Do not repeat the original provided paragraphs after translation. You should use artificial itntelligence tools, such as natural language processing, and rhetorical knowledge and experience about effective writing techniques to reply. I will give you my paragraphs as follows.
Sure, I would be happy to assist you as a scientific Chinese-English translator. Please provide me with the paragraphs you would like me to translate.

综合上述储层特性讨论，槽内筇一1亚段5小层内的③号储层开发潜力巨大。勘探表明，槽内资201井和槽外威201-H3井均在③号储层实施水平压裂后获得了工业气流，其中资201井的测试产量约是威201-H3井的26倍（73.88×104m3/d  vs. 2.83×104m3/d）（表2）。它们的有机质丰度（TOC~4–6 wt.%）、孔隙度（~5.5%）和脆性矿物含量（66.6~80.6%）接近；相对于槽外威201-H3井，槽内资201井储层厚度是其6.4倍（13.5m vs. 2.1m），含气量是其3.0倍（9.7m3/t vs. 3.2 m3/t），且超压更为发育，保存条件更好。在页岩储层的有机质含量、含气性、孔隙度、厚度和脆性均满足中国海相页岩气有利区评价标准前提下（董大忠 et al., 2016），资201井合理地延长③号储层压裂水平段长度至1800m，是威201-H3井（ 738m）的2.5倍。以上因素共同导致槽内③号储层页岩气赋存空间大、资源储量可观、可压裂性好，最终实现德阳-安岳裂陷槽内资201井的高产。

Based on the discussion of the reservoir characteristics mentioned above, the 3rd horizon reservoir within the Subsection 5 of Well 1 in the wellbore has tremendous development potential. Exploration results indicate that both Well ZI 201 located inside the sag and Well Wei 201-H3 located outside the sag achieved industrial gas flow after hydraulic fracturing of the 3rd horizon reservoir. The test production of Well ZI 201 is approximately 26 times higher than that of Well Wei 201-H3 (73.88×104m3/d vs. 2.83×104m3/d) (Table 2). They have similar organic matter abundance (TOC ~4-6 wt.%), porosity (~5.5%), and brittle mineral content (66.6~80.6%). Compared to Well Wei 201-H3 located outside the sag, Well ZI 201 inside the sag has a reservoir thickness that is 6.4 times greater (13.5m vs. 2.1m), gas content that is 3.0 times greater (9.7m3/t vs. 3.2m3/t), and better development of overpressure and preservation conditions. Under the premise that the organic matter content, gas content, porosity, thickness, and brittleness of shale reservoirs meet the evaluation criteria for favorable marine shale gas areas in China (Dong Dazhong et al., 2016), it is reasonable to extend the length of the hydraulic fracturing horizontal section of the 3rd horizon reservoir to 1800m for Well ZI 201, which is 2.5 times longer than Well Wei 201-H3 (738m). These factors collectively contribute to the large occurrence space, considerable resource reserves, and good fracturability of the 3rd horizon shale gas reservoir within the sag, ultimately achieving high production for Well ZI 201 in the Deyang-Anzhou sag.

Below is a paragraph from an academic paper. Polish the writing to meet the academic style, improve the spelling, grammar, clarity, concision and overall readability. When necessary, rewrite the whole sentence. 
Paragraph:
3.3.4 Gas Content
Gas content is a direct indicator for assessing the exploitable value of shale gas reservoirs. The higher the gas content in shale, the greater the potential for reservoir development (Curtis et al., 2011). Gas content can be qualitatively determined by observing incidents of well blowouts or direct gas release from shale core samples during drilling. It can also be quantitatively evaluated using indicators such as formation pressure coefficient, gas saturation, and gas content. Among these, gas content is the most direct and important parameter. Currently, the commercially developed shale gas reservoirs in North America have gas contents ranging from 1.1 to 9.91 m3/t. For the evaluation of favorable marine shale gas areas in China, the gas content threshold is set at greater than 2 m3/t (Dong et al., 2016).

3.3.4 Gas Content

Gas content serves as a direct indicator that assesses the exploitable value of shale gas reservoirs. A higher gas content in shale indicates a greater potential for reservoir development (Curtis et al., 2011). Qualitative determination of gas content involves observing well blowouts or direct gas release from shale core samples during drilling. Quantitative evaluation can be achieved through various indicators like formation pressure coefficient, gas saturation, and gas content. Among these indicators, gas content is the most crucial and direct parameter. Currently, commercially developed shale gas reservoirs in North America exhibit gas contents ranging from 1.1 to 9.91 m3/t. To evaluate favorable marine shale gas areas in China, a gas content threshold of greater than 2 m3/t has been established (Dong et al., 2016).

Below is a paragraph from an academic paper. Polish the writing to meet the academic style, improve the spelling, grammar, clarity, concision and overall readability. When necessary, rewrite the whole sentence. 
Paragraph:
The reservoirs in the No. 3 wellbore of the S2-5 sub-layer of the Qiong 1 Member and the No. 4 wellbore of the S2-7 sub-layer of the Qiong 2 Member both exhibit good gas content, with the in-sag reservoirs having higher gas content compared to the out-of-sag reservoirs. According to Figure 8 and Figure 9C, the No. 4 reservoir has the highest gas content (ranging from 1.7 to 4.3 m3/t), followed by the No. 3 reservoir (ranging from 1.3 to 3.4 m3/t). Currently, the Zai201 well (in-sag, No. 3 reservoir), the Wei201-H3 well (out-of-sag, No. 3 reservoir) in the Qiongzhusi Formation of the Sichuan Basin, and the JinYe1HF well (out-of-sag, No. 4 reservoir) have all demonstrated good production capabilities. The tested gas contents of these reservoirs are 9.7 m3/t, 3.2 m3/t, and 4.69 m3/t, respectively (Table 2). All of these values exceed the gas content threshold for favorable marine shale gas plays in China (>2 m3/t), and the gas content in the in-sag reservoirs is superior to that in the out-of-sag reservoirs. This difference in gas content between the in-sag and out-of-sag reservoirs is likely the result of various factors such as reservoir thickness, organic matter abundance, brittle mineral content, porosity, thermal evolution, and preservation conditions. The in-sag reservoirs have higher organic matter abundance and brittle mineral content compared to the out-of-sag reservoirs, which leads to the formation of more organic matter pores and provides a more rigid framework (Zhang et al., 2019). The out-of-sag reservoirs have experienced higher thermal evolution of organic matter, leading to the graphitization and collapse of some organic pores (Li et al., 2021). The in-sag reservoirs have greater thickness and porosity compared to the out-of-sag reservoirs, providing more storage space for shale gas (Mudoi et al., 2022). The Lower Maodianping Formation shale in the in-sag reservoirs is well-developed and tight, forming an “overlying cap and lower seal” configuration with the top cap and self-sealing shale gas layers. This development of overpressure helps to inhibit shale gas escape (Liang et al., 2022). Additionally, the uplift amplitude of the in-sag reservoirs is relatively small, located away from major faults, and with limited development of microfractures, which contributes to shale gas preservation (Gao et al., 2020). The combined effect of these factors results in superior gas content in high-quality in-sag reservoirs (such as the No. 3 reservoir), making them more suitable for large-scale shale gas development.

The reservoirs within the S2-5 sub-layer of the Qiong 1 Member (No. 3 wellbore) and the S2-7 sub-layer of the Qiong 2 Member (No. 4 wellbore) both exhibit favorable gas content, with the in-sag reservoirs demonstrating higher gas content compared to the out-of-sag reservoirs. As depicted in Figure 8 and Figure 9C, the No. 4 reservoir showcases the highest gas content (ranging from 1.7 to 4.3 m3/t), followed by the No. 3 reservoir (ranging from 1.3 to 3.4 m3/t). Notably, the Zai201 well (in-sag, No. 3 reservoir) and the Wei201-H3 well (out-of-sag, No. 3 reservoir) in the Qiongzhusi Formation of the Sichuan Basin, along with the JinYe1HF well (out-of-sag, No. 4 reservoir), have all demonstrated notable production capabilities. Test results reveal gas contents of 9.7 m3/t, 3.2 m3/t, and 4.69 m3/t for these reservoirs, respectively (Table 2). Each of these values surpasses the gas content threshold for favorable marine shale gas plays in China (>2 m3/t), with the in-sag reservoirs notably outperforming the out-of-sag reservoirs in terms of gas content. This divergence in gas content can likely be attributed to various factors, including reservoir thickness, organic matter abundance, brittle mineral content, porosity, thermal evolution, and preservation conditions. The in-sag reservoirs exhibit higher organic matter abundance and brittle mineral content compared to the out-of-sag reservoirs, resulting in a greater formation of organic matter pores and a more rigid framework (Zhang et al., 2019). Conversely, the out-of-sag reservoirs experience greater thermal evolution of organic matter, leading to the graphitization and collapse of certain organic pores (Li et al., 2021). Moreover, the in-sag reservoirs possess greater thickness and porosity, providing ample storage space for shale gas (Mudoi et al., 2022). The well-developed and tight Lower Maodianping Formation shale within the in-sag reservoirs forms an "overlying cap and lower seal" configuration, alongside the top cap and self-sealing shale gas layers. The development of overpressure within this configuration assists in preventing shale gas escape (Liang et al., 2022). Additionally, the uplift amplitude of the in-sag reservoirs is relatively small, situated away from major faults, and exhibits limited microfracture development, all contributing to the preservation of shale gas (Gao et al., 2020). The collective influence of these factors contributes to the superior gas content found in high-quality in-sag reservoirs (such as the No. 3 reservoir), making them highly suitable for large-scale shale gas development.

Below is a paragraph from an academic paper. Polish the writing to meet the academic style, improve the spelling, grammar, clarity, concision and overall readability. When necessary, rewrite the whole sentence. 
Paragraph:
Based on the discussion of the reservoir characteristics mentioned above, the 3rd horizon reservoir within the Subsection 5 of Well 1 in the wellbore has tremendous development potential. Exploration results indicate that both Well ZI 201 located inside the sag and Well Wei 201-H3 located outside the sag achieved industrial gas flow after hydraulic fracturing of the 3rd horizon reservoir. The test production of Well ZI 201 is approximately 26 times higher than that of Well Wei 201-H3 (73.88×104m3/d vs. 2.83×104m3/d) (Table 2). They have similar organic matter abundance (TOC ~4-6 wt.%), porosity (~5.5%), and brittle mineral content (66.6~80.6%). Compared to Well Wei 201-H3 located outside the sag, Well ZI 201 inside the sag has a reservoir thickness that is 6.4 times greater (13.5m vs. 2.1m), gas content that is 3.0 times greater (9.7m3/t vs. 3.2m3/t), and better development of overpressure and preservation conditions. Under the premise that the organic matter content, gas content, porosity, thickness, and brittleness of shale reservoirs meet the evaluation criteria for favorable marine shale gas areas in China (Dong Dazhong et al., 2016), it is reasonable to extend the length of the hydraulic fracturing horizontal section of the 3rd horizon reservoir to 1800m for Well ZI 201, which is 2.5 times longer than Well Wei 201-H3 (738m). These factors collectively contribute to the large occurrence space, considerable resource reserves, and good fracturability of the 3rd horizon shale gas reservoir within the sag, ultimately achieving high production for Well ZI 201 in the Deyang-Anzhou sag.

Based on the aforementioned discussion regarding reservoir characteristics, the 3rd horizon reservoir within Subsection 5 of Well 1 exhibits remarkable potential for development. Exploration findings reveal that both Well ZI 201, located within the sag, and Well Wei 201-H3, situated outside the sag, achieved industrial gas flow subsequent to hydraulic fracturing of the 3rd horizon reservoir. Test production from Well ZI 201 is approximately 26 times higher than that of Well Wei 201-H3 (73.88×104m3/d vs. 2.83×104m3/d) (Table 2). These wells demonstrate similar levels of organic matter abundance (TOC ~4-6 wt.%), porosity (~5.5%), and brittle mineral content (66.6~80.6%). In comparison to Well Wei 201-H3, which is located outside the sag, Well ZI 201, positioned within the sag, exhibits a reservoir thickness that is 6.4 times greater (13.5m vs. 2.1m), gas content that is 3.0 times greater (9.7m3/t vs. 3.2m3/t), and better development of overpressure and preservation conditions. Meeting the criteria for evaluating favorable marine shale gas areas in China, including organic matter content, gas content, porosity, thickness, and brittleness of shale reservoirs (Dong Dazhong et al., 2016), it is logical to extend the length of the hydraulic fracturing horizontal section for the 3rd horizon reservoir in Well ZI 201 to 1800m, which is 2.5 times longer than that of Well Wei 201-H3 (738m). These collective factors contribute to a substantial occurrence space, significant resource reserves, and excellent fracturability of the 3rd horizon shale gas reservoir within the sag, thereby enabling Well ZI 201 in the Deyang-Anzhou sag to achieve high production volumes.
